docs.title: 🤖 Gen AI Notes
docs.text:
"Artificial intelligence is one of the most profound things we're working on as humanity. It's more profound than fire or electricity." — Sundar Pichai (CEO, Google)

Welcome to Gen AI Notes — a structured, beginner-to-advanced reference for Generative AI, covering everything from the math foundations to LLMs, RAG pipelines, AI Agents, and production deployment.

docs.title: 🗺️ What's Inside
docs.text: # Section What You'll Learn 01 🧱 Foundations What Gen AI is, its history, and how it differs from traditional AI/ML 02 📐 Math & ML Basics Linear algebra, probability, backpropagation, and neural networks 03 🧠 Deep Learning CNNs, RNNs, LSTMs, and the attention mechanism 04 ⚡ Transformers The architecture behind modern AI — BERT, GPT, ViT 05 🗣️ Large Language Models How LLMs are pretrained, tokenized, and scaled 06 💬 Prompt Engineering CoT, ReAct, few-shot prompting, and prompt security 07 🎛️ Fine-Tuning LoRA, QLoRA, RLHF, DPO, and instruction tuning 08 🔍 Embeddings & Vector Search Embeddings, vector databases, and similarity search 09 📦 RAG Retrieval-Augmented Generation from naive to modular 10 🤖 AI Agents Tool use, memory, planning, and multi-agent systems 11 🛠️ Frameworks & Tools LangChain, LlamaIndex, LangGraph, HuggingFace, Ollama 12 🎨 Multimodal AI Text-to-image, video, audio, and vision-language models 13 🌊 Diffusion Models DDPM, Stable Diffusion, ControlNet 14 📊 Evaluation & Benchmarks Metrics, hallucination detection, MMLU, HumanEval 15 🚀 Deployment & MLOps Quantization, distillation, inference optimization, LLMOps 16 🛡️ Safety & Alignment Bias, red teaming, guardrails, and responsible AI 17 💼 Use Cases Chatbots, code generation, summarization, enterprise AI

docs.title: 🚀 How to Use These Notes
docs.text:
New to Gen AI?

Start with Foundations → Math & ML Basics → Deep Learning → Transformers before jumping into LLMs.

Already familiar with the basics?

Jump straight to Prompt Engineering, RAG, or AI Agents depending on what you're building.

Keep in mind

Gen AI is a fast-moving field. These notes focus on core concepts and fundamentals that remain relevant regardless of which model or tool is trending.

docs.title: 🛠️ Run Locally
docs.text:
# Clone the repo
git clone https://github.com/yxshkm-notes/genai-notes.git
cd genai-notes

# Create virtual environment
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate   # Mac/Linux
venv\Scripts\activate      # Windows

# Install dependencies
pip install mkdocs mkdocs-material

# Serve locally
mkdocs serve

Open http://127.0.0.1:8000 in your browser.

docs.title: 🤝 Contributing
docs.text:
Found a mistake or want to add something? Contributions are welcome!

  • Check the README for contribution steps
  • Open an Issue to report problems
  • Submit a Pull Request with your improvements

⭐ If you find this helpful, give it a star on GitHub — it keeps the motivation going!
